commands.repair_reason: This needs maintainer product/security review plus contributor real behavior proof before any automated repair lane would be useful.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete Slack config-boundary concern by allowing event-driven channel allowlist writes without honoring the documented configWrites gate.; Review finding: [P1] Honor configWrites before mutating Slack config — extensions/slack/src/monitor/event... (sha=70033bcf0cdd6b463232c255fdfdeceb75da3aba)
commands.review_summary:
Adds Slack member-join handling that auto-writes a channel allowlist entry when an allowlisted user invites OpenClaw into a channel, plus tests and a changelog entry.

PR surface: Source +158, Tests +76, Docs +1. Total +235 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: no. for runtime proof; source inspection shows the new event path and mocked tests, but the PR does not include a real Slack invite/config-write reproduction. The missing real behavior proof is especially important because this changes persisted config from a Slack event.
commands.review_followup: This needs maintainer product/security review plus contributor real behavior proof before any automated repair lane would be useful.
